allow:
Bash(this all of it?" question — the bar updates live as filters, scrubbers
and selections change, with a contextual hint slot \("123 geolocated",
"Loading device tracks", "Server reports 8497 — 2103 after dedupe"\).

Architecture: a CellebriteStatusProvider wraps all tabs in
CellebriteView. Each tab calls useCellebriteStatus\({ isActive, total,
displayed, selected, label, hint }\) — the active tab publishes; hidden
tabs are gated so they don''t clobber the visible one. CellebriteStatusBar
mounts once at the bottom of the shell and renders whatever the active
tab last published, with a muted "—" before the first publish.

Wired into Overview, Comms Center and Events Center as the high-traffic
surfaces. Other Cellebrite tabs can adopt the hook later — the bar
gracefully shows muted dashes until they do.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(tile → right rail with the rows it contains" UX.

Both honour the same case_id / report_keys / start_date / end_date
filter contract every other Cellebrite endpoint uses, plus an
optional bbox on the tiles call so panning the map only aggregates
the visible area. Uses the case_id and \(case_id,
cellebrite_report_key\) indexes shipped in fbe8df0 — no extra schema.

Frontend helpers added to cellebriteEventsAPI but no UI consumer
yet; the Locations tab continues to use raw-point rendering. The
tab will switch to tiles in the next commit \(G3\) along with the
rail integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(slow" reports without re-deriving the diagnostic flow each time.

Five tiers, run in order, measure-first posture:

  Tier 0 — verify deploy state \(HEAD commit, services up, free,
           df, journal for OOM/tracebacks\)
  Tier 1 — identify the slow request \(browser network tab or
           server-side curl -w timings against the case-id\)
  Tier 2 — narrow to layer \(Python projection vs Neo4j Cypher
           vs JSON serialise/transit\), with EXPLAIN/PROFILE Cypher
           commands and SHOW INDEXES for index-coverage check
  Tier 3 — memory-pressure diagnostics \(dmesg OOM, ps RSS, watch
           backend RSS over time during repro\)
  Tier 4 — Cellebrite-specific quick checks \(reconciliation,
           geocoder status, per-phone event counts\)
  Tier 5 — escalation rules: when to STOP and report rather than
           silently restart / bump a limit

Includes a Phase A–D commit map so the on-box Claude doesn''t
mis-attribute slowness to features it didn''t cause. None of the
shipped tab-scoped endpoints run on case open after d9b0e91, so
"case is slow on open" should not blame them without proof.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(hard to work in" because the conversation timeline at the bottom and
the From/To filter at the top kept it squeezed.

Reusable shared/ResizableSplit primitive:
- 6px draggable divider with hover affordance + grab cursor
- pointermove/up live on document so the drag tracks even when the
  cursor leaves the divider
- Per-case localStorage persistence \(keys like cb.comms.bottomTimeline.{caseId}\)
  so investigators get their preferred ratio back next session
- Vertical or horizontal direction; min/max bounds enforced

Applied to:
- Comms Center: thread-list / thread-view horizontal split + the
  cross-type timeline at the bottom is now drag-resizable upward,
  giving the message feed as much vertical room as the user wants
- Locations: map / table vertical split \(was fixed h-64 table\)
- Events Center: map / table vertical split for the ''split'' viewMode

Plan file extended with Phase H \(section 12\) covering primitive design,
per-tab application, and what we deferred \(horizontal splits in
Locations, named layout presets, drag-to-fully-collapse\).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
